---
title: "Input Language and Execution"
description: "Understand the statement-oriented OPALX input language and execution model."
audience: user
status: experimental
last-reviewed: "2026-07-22"
---

OPALX reads a MAD-inspired, statement-oriented language. Statements end with a
semicolon, names bind commands or objects, and expressions may use constants
and variables.

```opal
TITLE, STRING="Minimal beamline study";

REAL cell_length = 1.0;
D1: DRIFT, L=cell_length;
CELL: LINE=(D1);

TRACK, LINE=CELL, BEAM=BEAM1, MAXSTEPS={10}, DT={1.0e-11};
  RUN, METHOD="PARALLEL", FIELDSOLVER=FS1;
ENDTRACK;
QUIT;
```

The example shows the language shape, not a complete runnable model: a beam,
distribution, source list, and field solver must also be defined. Use a current
regression input as the starting point for an executable case.

## Statement classes

- **Actions** perform work, change parser state, or end execution.
- **Definitions** create beams, distributions, field solvers, values, and
  related objects.
- **Elements** describe fields, diagnostics, markers, and beamline geometry.
- **Lines** assemble named elements into the tracked lattice.
- **Tracking blocks** select a line and beam and execute the parallel tracker.

The [Command Reference](../reference/commands.qmd) lists objects registered by
the current executable.
